summary: Read-only R8 install/new-PC/release equivalence review completed. Inspected Codex memory registry DinoBrain entries, accepted DinoBrain memory records, completion/consensus docs, install.ps1, Windows installer/release scripts, package scripts, verifier scripts, and current local artifact/version state. Verdict: the consensus plan states adequate completion conditions, but current implementation/proof is not yet sufficient to claim full install/new-PC/release equivalence because release manifest/install-equivalence verifiers are missing from package/verify:goal, Git-missing fallback can pass partial verification, release manifest artifact is absent, and current artifacts/repo state show drift risks.
decisions: Treat docs/OS_REMAINING_GAPS_CONSENSUS_PLAN.md P2-12 as directionally sufficient because it requires new-PC hard gates after Codex/Claude registration, release manifest fields, live hook-trust evidence, and degraded-only Git-missing ZIP fallback.

Do not mark current DinoBrain install/release equivalence complete: package.json lacks verify:install-equivalence and verify:release-manifest scripts, and scripts/verify-closed-loop-goal.mjs does not gate installer/release equivalence.

Treat install.ps1 as partially strong: it clones/updates app and data repos, checks git ref drift when Git exists, installs portable Node, builds/indexes, registers Codex/Claude hooks and MCP where available, and creates Observatory/diagnostic/live-proof/uninstall launchers.

Treat Git-missing ZIP fallback as risky because installer verification allows DINOBRAIN_ALLOW_NO_GIT partial verification and version-alignment verifier exits 0 when Git is missing; this needs a hard degraded/full-equivalence distinction.

Treat current release artifacts as not completion-grade evidence: ZIP SHA file matches current ZIP, but loose EXE SHA file is stale against current EXE, HEAD has no tag, app/data HEADs differ from older memory snapshot, data repo is dirty, and no release manifest artifact ties app commit/data commit/tag/assets/verifier statuses together.
next_steps: Add explicit verify:install-equivalence and verify:release-manifest commands, then include them in verify:goal and the release readiness checklist.

Generate a release manifest/proof artifact containing app commit/branch, data commit/branch, installer version, package version, ZIP/SHA, tag target, GitHub asset id/name/size/hash when uploaded, and all required verifier statuses.
Make Git-missing ZIP fallback return degraded mode that fails full equivalence even if partial installer verification can proceed.
Update local packaging to regenerate or remove stale loose EXE SHA, and require ZIP/SHA/tag/GitHub asset parity before release readiness claims.
Run a fresh install/update rehearsal on a clean temp or new-PC-like environment with Git present, and separately prove the Git-missing degraded path fails full equivalence.
